$comment:
Per-secondary-entry-point size budget for the SHIPPED Caelum library (Book 18 §3.2 — the OTHER size gate). Measured by scripts/check-lib-size.mjs as the gzipped byte size of each dist/caelum/fesm2022/*.mjs. angular.json `budgets` measure only the Forge APPLICATION build (esbuild), NOT ng-packagr output — hence this bespoke gate. It defends the 'pay only for what you import' contract: importing cae-select must not drag in cae-tree. A NEW entry point with no budget row here FAILS the gate — set its budget deliberately (the forward ratchet). Since #28 the monolith is split: `caelum` is now a THIN re-export barrel (it re-exports the per-component entry points by name, so it carries no component code — ~0.7kb of re-export shims) and every `caelum-` row below is one tree-shakable component — EXCEPT `caelum-form-field`, a runtime abstract-directive base (`CaeFormFieldControlBase`, #46/#135, generic over its value type) shared by the five form-field controls (cae-input/textarea/select/autocomplete/multi-select): like `caelum-shared` it is not a component, but unlike it, it carries runtime code. Budgets carry ~15-30% headroom over the measured size so a refactor doesn't trip the gate, but real growth (e.g. #29 adds error-forwarding to input) BUMPS the affected row deliberately; a new component/adapter adds its own row. Extracting shared code (#46 moved the CVA + error-forwarding out of input/select/textarea into caelum-form-field) SHRINKS the source rows — retighten them to the new measurement. Two rows are deliberately TIGHT because their size is itself an invariant: `caelum` (the barrel) must stay a thin re-export shim — a budget near its measured ~0.7kb catches a bad merge that inlines a component into it; `caelum-shared` must stay type-only (near-zero runtime) — a tight budget trips the moment a real runtime symbol is added to it. Book 18 §4b illustrative targets: caelum-select ~12kb, caelum-data-grid ~40kb (the M2 adapters land far heavier than these thin Direct wrappers) — but caelum-grid measures ~13kb gzip, NOT ~40kb, because the TanStack engine is left EXTERNAL (an OPTIONAL peerDependency `@tanstack/table-core`, imported by grid.adapter.ts, not inlined into the fesm), so the ~14kb engine cost is paid once by the consumer's app build ONLY when they opt into provideTanStackGrid. Client-default consumers ship zero engine bytes VIA STANDARD TREE-SHAKING (both `caelum` and `@tanstack/table-core` declare sideEffects:false, so an unreferenced re-export is dropped) — this measured budget guards the fesm, and Forge proves the DEFERRAL path (table-core verified absent from the eager chunk); a dedicated consumer-tree-shake bundling guard is filed as a followup. The #171 bump 11264->16384 covers the TanStack adapter's own source + JSDoc, not the engine. The #176 bump 16384->20480 covers the dependency-free ServerGridAdapter (the lazy/remote engine, `server-grid-adapter.ts`) — its own source + JSDoc, no engine (it imports no grid library, like the client default). The #192 bump 20480->24576 covers the loading-blocks-interaction + [caeGridBusy] slot work on `data-grid.ts` (aria-disabled sort/pager, the busy overlay, and the expanded [loading] JSDoc that ships in the fesm) — measured ~19kb gzip, restoring ~20% headroom. The #202 bump caelum-chip-set 5120->6656 covers the optional [emptyFocusTarget] empty-set focus hook on `chip-set.ts` (the input + onRemoved handler + the review-expanded JSDoc that ships in the fesm — gate/sync-drop/live-region caveats) — measured ~5.4kb gzip, restoring ~19% headroom. The #143 bump caelum-table 6400->10240 covers the custom cell-template seam (`cell-def.ts`: the CaeCellDef directive + CaeCellContext, the NgTemplateOutlet per-cell rendering with template-or-text fallback, the typo/duplicate dev-warn effect, and the p-table-parity + accessible-cell-content JSDoc that ships in the fesm) — measured ~7.7kb gzip, restoring ~25% headroom. The #144 bump caelum-table 10240->11264 covers row selection (selectionMode='none'|'multiple' + a vendor-neutral [(selection)] model + the prepended select-all/row mat-checkbox column + selectedSet/allSelected/someSelected/toggleRow/toggleAll + rowSelectionLabel/selectAllLabel a11y accessors + the reserved-key validateConfig guard + JSDoc; MatCheckbox stays external, not inlined) — measured ~9.3kb gzip, restoring ~18% headroom. The #213 bump caelum-table 11264->12288 covers the absolute row-index context field (a CaeCellContext.absoluteIndex + the pageOffset signal tracked off the paginator's page event + JSDoc) — measured ~9.9kb gzip, restoring ~17% headroom. The #144 single-select bump caelum-table 12288->15360 covers single-select mode (selectionMode='single' + a radio column via MatRadioModule [external, not inlined — only the template branch/selectOne/radioName/JSDoc add bytes], the shared-name native radio-group a11y, a self-managed roving [tabIndex] so the group is one tab stop not N, a visually-hidden selection-column header for AT, and the review-expanded a11y JSDoc) — measured ~12.2kb gzip, restoring ~18% headroom. The #224 bump caelum-table 15360->16384 covers opt-in click/Space-to-deselect for single-select (the `allowDeselect` input + the `onRadioActivate` re-activation handler + the two `(click)`/`(keydown.space)` radio bindings + the deviation JSDoc that ships in the fesm) — measured ~13.0kb gzip, restoring ~18% headroom. The #194 bump caelum-grid 24576->28672 covers the born-empty status live region (a `rendered` signal + afterNextRender, and the clip-based visually-hidden empty state that keeps role=status registered in the a11y tree instead of display:none) + JSDoc — measured ~22.3kb gzip, restoring ~22% headroom. The #144 expandable-rows bump caelum-table 16384->20480 covers expandable detail rows (a vendor-neutral [(expanded)] model + the prepended accessible expand-toggle column via MatButtonModule [mat-icon-button, bundled into this fesm] with the aria-expanded/aria-controls disclosure pattern + the multiTemplateDataRows full-width detail row with a projected `caeRowDetailDef` template (`row-detail-def.ts`: the CaeRowDetailDef directive + CaeRowDetailContext) + isExpanded/toggleExpand/detailId WeakMap + the expand/detail reserved-key guard + the a11y JSDoc that ships in the fesm) — measured ~16.2kb gzip, restoring ~19% headroom. The #144 sticky bump caelum-table 20480->24576 covers sticky header + sticky columns (the `stickyHeader` input + the `sticky`/`stickyEnd` pass-throughs on each column def + the `leadColumnSticky` computed that auto-freezes the built-in expand/select columns behind a frozen leading data column [#252, a WCAG 2.4.11 focus-occlusion fix] + the reactive `warnOnStickyConfig` dev-guards for both-edges and non-contiguous sticky runs, whose warning STRINGS ship in the fesm because the isDevMode() gate is a runtime check + the sticky/scroll-context a11y JSDoc) — no CSS of our own (Material's .mat-mdc-table-sticky supplies position:sticky and the inherited opaque background) — measured ~20.0kb gzip, restoring ~17% headroom. The #273 NEW row caelum-carousel 11264 is the first ★ media-family entry point (Book 11 §3.4) — a content-agnostic rotating carousel: the signal sliding-window index model + circular/autoplay(pause-on-hover/focus, WCAG 2.2.2 play/pause, prefers-reduced-motion no-autostart) + prev/next + roving-tabindex indicator group + full APG carousel ARIA + the caeCarouselItem projected-slide directive + its inline CSS-drawn glyphs (no icon font) + JSDoc; NgTemplateOutlet is the only Angular import, no Material — measured ~9.5kb gzip, ~16% headroom (2-lens review dropped the inert responsiveOptions input, switched indicator focus to a viewChildren query, and added the aria-disabled-nav + numScroll-clamp + SSR-timer-guard fixes). The #279 NEW row caelum-tree-select 8704 is the ★ value-picker family's tree control (Book 09 §3.5, `p-treeSelect` parity) — a hierarchical single/multi node-select whose panel is the library's FIRST cdkConnectedOverlay: a mat-tree (rendering + roving-tabindex + expand/collapse from the CDK tree, like cae-tree) + a selection Set + cdkTrapFocus (focus-in on open, restore-to-trigger on dismiss); value = node key(s) via NG_VALUE_ACCESSOR (the #47 consumer-owned-error pattern for selection controls, NOT the mat-form-field base — a custom overlay has no inner Material control to bridge), reusing the CaeTreeNode model type-only (erased, no bundle coupling); A11yModule + OverlayModule + MatTreeModule stay external, not inlined — measured ~8.0kb gzip after the 2-lens review (8704->9728, ~16% headroom) — the review shipped the aria-controls-to-the-role=tree fix, single-mode aria-selected-only-on-selected, an empty-tree focus-strand guard, setDisabledState-without-touched, a selectionMode set-once dev-warn, and a single-select no-op re-emit guard (deferring focus-selected-on-open #281, multi-select live region #281, showClear/filter/dup-key-warn #282, lazy/virtual/mat-form-field #283). The caelum barrel row 960->1024 absorbs the tree-select re-export shim (+3 B — legitimate thin-shim growth, still tight enough to catch a bad component inline, which would add KBs not bytes). The #274 NEW row caelum-galleria 9472 (measured ~7.8kb gzip after the 2-lens review, ~15% headroom) is the ★ media family's image gallery (Book 11 §3.4, p-galleria parity) — a main image view + prev/next navigators + a thumbnail tablist (WAI-ARIA tabs, roving tabindex, selection-follows-focus) + a polite position live region + a fullscreen lightbox opened through caelum/dialog (D-15): the internal CaeGalleriaLightbox component (large image + prev/next + counter + Escape/backdrop close, all on Material's centered modal) ships in this fesm, but @angular/material/dialog and caelum/dialog stay EXTERNAL (imported, not inlined). v1 renders the full thumbnail strip (fully keyboard-verifiable); cdk-virtual-scroll virtualization for very large galleries is deferred to a follow-up gated on the #240 browser runner. The barrel row stays 1024 (the +3 B galleria re-export shim fits its existing headroom). The #275 NEW row caelum-image (the ★ media family's smallest member, Book 11 §3.4, p-image parity) is a token-styled image with a click-to-preview trigger + a fullscreen zoom/rotate/pan lightbox opened through caelum/dialog (D-15): the internal CaeImagePreview component (image + zoom/rotate/reset/close toolbar + a keyboard arrow-pan path + pointer drag-pan + a polite zoom live region, on Material's centered modal) ships in this fesm, but @angular/material/dialog and caelum/dialog stay EXTERNAL (imported, not inlined). The zoom/pan CSS-transform primitive here is what the galleria lightbox will later share (#289); the before/after cae-image-compare slider is its own slice (#293). Measured ~7.5kb gzip after the 2-lens review (8192->9216, ~83% headroom) — the review flipped preview to default-off (p-image parity), added a figure/figcaption caption association, renamed the reset control to 'Reset view' (it clears rotation+pan too), normalized rotation mod 360, and added an isDevMode zoom-bounds guard (its warn STRING ships in the fesm because isDevMode() is a runtime check). The barrel row stays 1024 (the +image re-export shim fits its existing headroom). The #301 NEW row caelum-input-number 5120 is the first M3 input-family entry point (Book 08 §3.1, p-inputNumber parity) — the dedicated numeric text input: a number|null CVA over Intl.NumberFormat extending CaeFormFieldControlBase (the mat-form-field base stays EXTERNAL, not inlined), with the model/view split (canonical number model + a separate formatted view-string signal), a locale-aware parse (grouping-strip + decimal-normalize + sign/lone-'-' handling), currency mode + a dev-warn currency-code fallback (its warn STRING ships in the fesm because isDevMode() is a runtime check), and min/max fraction digits + JSDoc. The 5120->6144 bump absorbs the 2-lens review fixes (all shipping in the fesm): a runtime-reformat effect (Finding 1 — reformat the view when a formatting input changes while unfocused, so a currency/locale switch can't leave a stale display), a blur snap-to-displayed-precision (Finding 2 — WYSIWYG, p-inputNumber parity), a min>max fraction-digit clamp+dev-warn (Finding 3 — its warn STRING ships because isDevMode() is a runtime check), a NaN->empty writeValue coerce, and an overridable inputMode input (a11y — the decimal pad hides the minus key) + the expanded JSDoc — measured ~5.0kb gzip post-review, ~19% headroom. The barrel row stays 1024 (the +input-number re-export shim fits its existing headroom). The #303 NEW row caelum-input-otp 7168 is the second M3 input-family entry point (Book 08 §3.4, p-inputOtp parity) — the one-time-code input: N single-character cells presenting as one `string` CVA value via NG_VALUE_ACCESSOR (the #47 consumer-owned-error selection-control family, NOT the mat-form-field base — there is no single inner matInput to bridge across N cells), with a self-managed roving tabindex (one tab stop, focus moved by index via a viewChildren query — the deliberate no-FocusKeyManager choice, since the cells are native inputs the user types into and the manager's typeahead would hijack the digit keys) + paste/SMS-autofill spread + a length<1 dev-warn (its warn STRING ships in the fesm because isDevMode() is a runtime check) + token-only cell styling + JSDoc — measured ~6.0kb gzip, ~18% headroom. The 7168->8192 bump absorbs the 2-lens a11y/API review fixes (all shipping in the fesm): an activeTabStop clamp computed (the tab stop stays valid even if length shrinks past the active cell — the a11y MAJOR guard), a set-once dev-warn effect on runtime [length] change (its warn STRING ships because isDevMode() is a runtime check — closes the value/tab-stop-desync MAJOR by contract), a no-accessible-name dev-warn, a numberAttribute transform on length, dropping maxlength on cell 0 (so OS one-time-code autofill reaches onInput intact to spread), and the expanded JSDoc — measured ~6.9kb gzip post-review, ~16% headroom. The barrel row (989 B measured vs 1024) absorbs the +input-otp re-export shim within its existing headroom. The #304 NEW row caelum-password 6144 is the third M3 input-family entry point (Book 08 §3.5, p-password parity) — a password field extending CaeFormFieldControlBase (the mat-form-field base stays EXTERNAL, not inlined): the CVA value is the password `string` itself (no model/view split — flipping type password<->text never transforms it), plus an inline-SVG visibility toggle (a matSuffix button, self-authored eye/slashed-eye glyphs — NOT mat-icon, whose font ships from a Google CDN Caelum avoids for US-origin; aria-pressed conveys state under a constant accessible name) and an ADVISORY strength meter (a module-local 0-4 heuristic over length + character-class variety, rendered as a 4-segment bar whose error->warn->primary->success ramp reads from --cae-color-* only [Book 04 §3.6, no hardcoded colours], with a polite role=status text label so strength is never colour-only per WCAG 1.4.1 — the ENFORCED policy stays a form ValidatorFn, Book 07 §3.2; no scoring library pulled in, Book 03/D-11) + JSDoc — measured ~5.1kb gzip. The 6144->7168 bump absorbs the 2-lens a11y/API review fixes (all shipping in the fesm): IME composition buffering (a composing flag + compositionstart/end handlers, mirroring cae-input's family invariant — matters once the field is revealed to type=text, which re-enables the IME browsers suppress for a password; the API MAJOR), a context-prefixed strength live region ('Password strength: X' so the bare adjective can't be mistaken for the enforced error when read by a screen reader out of context — the a11y MAJOR), a no-accessible-name dev-warn (its warn STRING ships because isDevMode() is a runtime check), visible-state coherence (the [type] is guarded by toggleMask() so removing the toggle while revealed can't strand the field as plaintext, and writeValue re-masks so a programmatically-written secret never renders in the clear), the removal of a dead strengthId/[id] (both lenses flagged the dangling id — role=status already announces), and the expanded JSDoc — measured ~5.7kb gzip post-review, ~19% headroom. The barrel row stays 1024 (the +password re-export shim, 995 B measured, fits its existing headroom). The #302 NEW row caelum-input-mask 6144 is the FOURTH and last M3 input-family entry point (Book 08 §3.2, p-inputMask parity) — the fixed-template masked text input: a component extending CaeFormFieldControlBase (the mat-form-field base stays EXTERNAL, not inlined) whose CVA value is the UNMASKED string (the model/view split, Book 08 §2.2 — the form sees '2125550142', the inner matInput shows '(212) 555-0142'), with a ~90-line local mask engine (parseMask + conform + caretForDataIndex; no foreign mask library, Book 08 §2.3/D-11) driving live re-masking on every keystroke, caret-after-next-editable-slot discipline (the native value is managed imperatively via a viewChild ElementRef + setSelectionRange, NOT a [value] binding whose async re-write would clobber the caret, so unlike input-number's blur-only reformat it reformats mid-typing), paste-spread (a pasted decorated string conforms identically to typed digits), IME composition buffering (a composing flag skips mid-composition input, mirroring the family invariant), an auto inputmode='numeric' for all-digit masks + override, an optional keepLiteral (commit the decorated view instead), and a no-accessible-name dev-warn (its warn STRING ships in the fesm because isDevMode() is a runtime check) + JSDoc — measured ~5.7kb gzip pre-review. The 7168->8192 bump absorbs the 2-lens a11y/API review fixes (all shipping in the fesm): a beforeinput-driven collapsed-delete handler (onBeforeInput — Backspace/Delete removes the nearest DATA character instead of dead-ending on an immovable literal, closing the mask-backspace no-op trap the a11y lens flagged MAJOR), an isComposing guard on onInput (drops a stray post-compositionend input some browsers fire — API NIT), a mask-literal-is-also-valid-token-data dev-warn (silent view/model divergence — API MINOR; its warn STRING ships because isDevMode() is a runtime check), a .trim() on the no-accessible-name check (whitespace-only name — a11y NIT), and the expanded a11y JSDoc (pass a hint describing the format so mat-form-field links it via aria-describedby) — measured ~6.9kb gzip post-review, ~14% headroom. Deferred parity extras (#315): placeholder slots, a structural caeMaskComplete validator, mid-field-literal backspace, backslash escaping, KeyFilter. The barrel row stays 1024 (the +input-mask re-export shim fits its existing headroom). The #293 NEW row caelum-image-compare 6144 is the ★ media family's niche member (Book 11 §3.4, p-imagecompare parity) — a before/after reveal slider split out of #275 onto a DISTINCT substrate (a reveal slider, not the cae-image dialog-lightbox), hence its own entry point rather than folding into caelum/image: two stacked (before/after src+alt inputs rendered internally, so they overlap exactly and the component owns the alt text) whose top layer is clipped by a clip-path computed, revealed by a divider that is the APG WINDOW-SPLITTER separator (Book 11 §3.2, the same a11y work cae-splitter will need) — a focusable role=separator with aria-orientation/valuenow/valuemin/valuemax + a full keyboard resize path (arrows nudge by step, Home/End snap, PageUp/Down coarse; Book 11 §3.5 non-negotiable #1) whose horizontal axis (drag + Left/Right) resolves through Directionality for RTL (Book 04 §3.5 — the FIRST Caelum component to inject @angular/cdk/bidi; clip-path inset() has no logical form so the hidden side is dir-resolved, while the divider uses logical inset-inline-start). The pointer drag is native Pointer Events + setPointerCapture (a reveal tracks a pointer POSITION, not a repositioned element, so cdkDrag would be the wrong heavier tool — Book 11 §3.5 #6 no-foreign-drag-lib satisfied by the platform), value is a model signal (zoneless, #3.5 #4) clamped [0,100], and a no-accessible-name dev-warn fires if [ariaLabel] is absent (its warn STRING ships in the fesm because isDevMode() is a runtime check). NO LiveAnnouncer (Book 11 §3.5 #2): aria-valuenow on the focusable separator is announced like a slider thumb on each keyboard change, so a parallel live region would double-announce (the ticket's 'if warranted' → not warranted). @angular/cdk/bidi stays external (imported, not inlined). Measured ~4.8kb gzip pre-review. The 6144->7168 bump absorbs the 2-lens a11y/API review fixes (all shipping in the fesm): a real dragging-state flag replacing the buttons&1 hover guard (API MAJOR — the old guard honored a cross-element drag passing over the divider and a non-primary-button press jumped the reveal; now pointerdown with button!==0 is ignored, dragging is set on primary-button pointerdown + an explicit focus() so the keyboard path works after a mouse drag, and cleared on pointerup/pointercancel), a NaN-safe clamp + a constructor normalize effect (API MINOR — an out-of-range or non-finite two-way write now self-heals the model back into [0,100] so a consumer reading value() sees the rendered position, not just the render), a locale-neutral aria-valuetext ('N%' instead of the English 'N% revealed'; a11y MINOR — aria-valuetext overrides aria-valuenow so the translated word would be announced in every locale; consumer-overridable formatter deferred to #318), a both-images-alt-empty dev-warn (a11y MINOR — a comparison with no alt on either image is invisible to AT; its warn STRING ships because isDevMode() is a runtime check), a non-positive-[step] dev-warn (API NIT), and a surface-halo on the focus ring (a11y NIT — keeps the outline visible over any image backdrop). Measured ~5.8kb gzip post-review, ~17% headroom. Deferred parity extras (#318): vertical orientation, click-the-track-to-jump, a content-projection variant, an i18n aria-valuetext formatter. The barrel row stays 1024 (the +image-compare re-export shim fits its existing headroom). The #323 NEW row caelum-splitter 8192 opens the Splitter family (Book 11 §3.2, p-splitter parity) — a keyboard-resizable multi-panel splitter: a container CaeSplitter + projected CaeSplitterPanel children (the tabs/stepper contentChildren idiom, each panel's content captured as a TemplateRef and interleaved with the dividers) laid out as flex panes in a horizontal|vertical [layout], separated by (N-1) dividers that REUSE the #293 APG window-splitter separator (focusable role=separator + aria-orientation [horizontal layout -> vertical separator, the #293-confirmed convention] + aria-valuenow/min/max + the full keyboard resize path: axis arrows RTL-flipped for horizontal, Home/End snap the leading pane to its min/max, PageUp/Down coarse) with native Pointer-Events + setPointerCapture resize (the #293 primary-button dragging-index guard, no foreign drag lib, Book 11 §3.5 #6) and RTL via Directionality (Book 04 §3.5). Live sizes in a signal seeded from [size] and normalized to 100, NaN-safe-clamped against each [minSize] across the adjacent pair on every resize (the #293 clamp lesson); a (resizeEnd) output emits the sizes array; NO LiveAnnouncer (the focused separator's aria-valuenow announces like a slider thumb, §3.5 #2). Dev-warns (< 2 panels; minSize sum > 100; a panel [size] below its own [minSize]; blank [ariaLabel]; non-positive [step]) whose STRINGS ship in the fesm because isDevMode() is a runtime check. @angular/cdk/bidi + NgTemplateOutlet stay external. The 8192->11264 budget covers the measured ~8.7kb gzip: the seed-computed/override-signal two-layer size model + the pure-computed seed that avoids the projection-timing seed bug + dynamic per-pair aria-valuemin/valuemax; PLUS the 2-lens (a11y+API) review fixes that all ship in the fesm — the enforceMinSizes water-fill that keeps the seeded layout (and so aria-valuenow) inside each [minSize] (the both-lens MAJOR: a bare [size] seed rendered a pane below its min and announced an ARIA-invalid triple), the range()-clamped valueNow for coherence even in the infeasible case, Page-keys-as-coarse-axis-arrows (fixing a vertical PageUp/ArrowUp direction clash), a setLeading changed-flag so resizeEnd only fires on a real change (no spurious emit at a clamp boundary), the three added dev-warns above, and expanded JSDoc — measured ~8.7kb gzip post-review, restoring ~21% headroom. The barrel row stays 1024 (the +splitter re-export shim fits its existing headroom). Deferred to #325: per-divider accessible names + aria-controls, pointer gutter-width offset accuracy, override positional-reorder. Deferred parity extras: state persistence, collapse/expand, px min/max, double-click-reset, gutterSize/step tuning, touch niceties. The #328 NEW row caelum-scroll-panel 5120 is the Splitter family's ScrollPanel sibling (Book 11 §3.2, p-scrollpanel parity, COMPARISON row 96) — a token-styled cross-browser scroll container built the LAZIEST-sufficient way the book prescribes: native overflow:auto + token scrollbars (Firefox scrollbar-width/color, WebKit ::-webkit-scrollbar*, all --cae-* tokens) with NO custom-scrollbar engine (D-11 clean). A11y is the parity leg: a native ResizeObserver (guarded for non-RO/SSR) over the host + a content wrapper drives an `overflowing` signal; when it overflows the host is tabindex=0 (axe scrollable-region-focusable) and, when named via [ariaLabel], a role=region — neither when it fits. CdkScrollable (hostDirective) registers it with the ScrollDispatcher so anchored overlays reposition on its scroll. A dev-warn (its STRING ships in the fesm because isDevMode() is a runtime check) nudges naming an overflowing panel. Measured ~3.6kb gzip pre-review. The 5120 budget covers the measured ~4.3kb gzip after the 2-lens (a11y+API) review fixes that all ship in the fesm: an isFocused signal + focus/blur host listeners + a tabIndex computed that keeps a focus-holding container at tabindex=-1 when its overflow clears (the a11y MAJOR — removing tabindex from the active element dumped focus to , WCAG 2.4.3), a surface-halo focus ring (WCAG 1.4.11, mirroring cae-splitter), and the Safari-Full-Keyboard-Access + horizontal-detection-limitation (#329) JSDoc caveats — restoring ~19% headroom. Deferred to #329: horizontal-only-nowrap post-mount overflow detection + animated-threshold hysteresis. The barrel row stays 1024 (the +scroll-panel re-export shim fits its existing headroom). The #332 NEW row caelum-breadcrumb 4096 is the M3 navigation-trail component (Book 09 §3.4, p-breadcrumb parity, COMPARISON row 119) — the laziest-sufficient build the book prescribes verbatim: a semantic
+
    with aria-current="page" on the current page and token-styled separators, NO overlay/CDK/menu machinery (Angular core only, no Material import). Data-driven [items] + optional [home] (unified into one crumbs computed so the LAST crumb of the combined trail is the current page, non-link, even on a home-only page), [separator] (default '/'), [ariaLabel] (default 'Breadcrumb'), (itemSelect) output; follows the cae-tab-menu precedent (data-driven nav, routerLink deferred). Measured ~3.1kb gzip pre-review. The 4096 budget covers the measured ~3.3kb gzip after the 2-lens (a11y+API) review — no BLOCKER; each lens raised one MAJOR, both fixed and shipping in the fesm: (a11y) separators are real aria-hidden glyphs rendered as text interpolation, NOT CSS ::before content — Chromium/WebKit expose pseudo-element content to the a11y tree, so ::before would announce 'slash' between every crumb (the WAI-ARIA APG technique renders the glyph silently); this also removed the JSON.stringify-into-CSS-custom-property injection surface entirely; (API/demo) real href crumbs tore down the SPA before the (itemSelect) echo could paint, so the Forge demo uses fragment urls to keep the interaction live. Also applied: the home+items unification (fixes the home-only no-current-page gap + a DRY duplication), an empty-crumbs guard (renders no landmark at all rather than an empty 'Breadcrumb' region), a whitespace-safe ariaLabel().trim() || 'Breadcrumb' default, and links underlined at rest (WCAG 1.4.1/F73 — the link affordance is not colour-only). Measured ~3.3kb gzip post-review, ~20% headroom. The barrel row 1024->1280 absorbs the breadcrumb re-export shim (the +6 B shim tipped the tight caelum barrel to 1030 B over its 1024 budget; the bump stays tight enough to catch a bad component-inline, which would add KBs not bytes). Deferred to #333: routerLink/routerLinkActive mode, an {item, originalEvent} (itemSelect) payload for click-interception, per-item icons, and long-trail overflow/collapse. The #336 NEW row caelum-order-list 7680 opens the Book 11 §3.3 drag-drop cluster (OrderList/PickList/FileUpload; p-orderList parity) — a keyboard-operable, drag-reorderable role=listbox: rows are role=option over cdkDropList+cdkDrag with a roving tabindex (one tab stop, the active index in a signal — the deliberate no-CDK-key-manager choice, cae-input-otp precedent, since the list is a data-driven @for with no child option directives), a control column of four move buttons (up/top/down/bottom) that reorder the active row via moveItemInArray, every move (button or drop) announced 'Moved to position N of M' via LiveAnnouncer (Book 11 §3.5 #2), a content-agnostic caeOrderListItem projected template (unknown-typed context, {{item}} fallback), and a [(value)] model + (reorder) output. @angular/cdk/drag-drop + @angular/cdk/a11y stay external (imported, not inlined); no foreign drag library (Book 03 / §3.5 #6). Measured ~5.4kb gzip pre-review. The 6656->7680 bump absorbs the 2-lens (a11y+API) review fixes that all ship in the fesm: the MAJOR both lenses converged on — move buttons use aria-disabled, NOT native [disabled], so a button that reaches a bound stays focusable instead of blurring the keyboard user to mid-reorder (guarded handlers + an [aria-disabled='true'] CSS state); a visually-hidden aria-describedby instructions span naming the reorder affordance (a11y MINOR); an activeIndex-clamp effect so an external list-shrink can't leave a stale index that resurrects on regrow (API MINOR); a corrected headline docstring example (signal [value]/(valueChange) two-way + $any(item) cast + a one-way-binding-is-uncontrolled warning — the doc MAJOR); and a non-interactive-row caveat (role=option forbids nested focusables). Measured ~6.2kb gzip post-review, ~17% headroom. RTL/Directionality is correctly NOT needed (§3.5 #3 is scoped to horizontal axes; this is a vertical list). Deferred to #336 follow-ons: multi-select, filter, per-item disabled, drag handle, custom trackBy, controls-position, virtualization (#240-gated). The barrel row stays 1280 (the +order-list re-export shim fits its existing headroom). #341 bumps caelum-order-list 12288 to 14336: this session's in-list filter + multi-select + header slot + controlsPosition (DOM-reordered controls, not CSS order, so tab order tracks visual order) grew it to ~12.1kb, and the new budget restores the ~15% headroom the tight 12288 had lost; per-item disabled (aria-disabled predicate) + drag handle (cdkDragHandle grip) #341 then grew it to ~13.6kb, bumping 14336 to 16384 to keep ~20% headroom; the trackBy/dataKey identity-keying seam (keyOf routing + input + docs) #341 then grew it to ~15.3kb, bumping 16384 to 18432; the 2-lens review fixes (roving-focus-follow effect + select-all key-map) then landed on top, measured ~16.1kb = ~13% headroom (order-list is now feature-complete bar #240-gated virtualize). | #337 cae-pick-list (M3, Book 11 §3.3 drag-drop cluster, 2nd of OrderList/PickList/FileUpload): two connected keyboard-operable role=listbox lists (p-pickList parity) over @angular/cdk/drag-drop + @angular/cdk/a11y only. Two cdkDropLists wired with cdkDropListConnectedTo; a control column between them (move-selected ->, move-all =>, <- move-selected, <= move-all) makes transfer fully keyboard-reachable; each list has its own roving-tabindex active row (signal, clamped), and every transfer is announced via LiveAnnouncer. Content-agnostic caePickListItem template shared by both lists (unknown-typed, $any(item) in the template); [(source)]/[(target)] two-way models + (transfer) output. Reuses cae-order-list's aria-disabled-at-bounds pattern: the move buttons use aria-disabled (NOT native [disabled]) so a button that empties its source stays focusable instead of blurring the keyboard user to (guarded handlers + [aria-disabled='true'] CSS). SCOPE (v1): transfer-only - within-list drag reorder is disabled (cdkDropListSortingDisabled) so the only drag op is the cross-list transfer, which has a full keyboard path; reordering WITHIN a list (drag + its own move buttons, shipped together) is deferred so section 3.5's keyboard-path-for-every-drag invariant is never broken. RTL of the transfer axis, multi-select, per-side header slots, and in-list filter are deferred follow-ons (#342). Measured ~7.75kb gzip; budget 9600 (~19% headroom). No foreign drag library (Book 03 / section 3.5 #6). Barrel row stays 1280 (the +pick-list re-export shim fits its existing headroom). cae-file-upload (#338): the third/final drag-drop-cluster member. Keyboard-reachable native input[type=file] + native DataTransfer dropzone (NOT cdkDropList — that transfers cdkDrag items, never OS file drops), trust-boundary type/size validation before upload, HttpClient reportProgress upload with per-file progress/cancel/retry, controlled CVA (value = accepted File[]), #47 ariaDescribedby + LiveAnnouncer. Angular core + @angular/common/http + @angular/cdk/a11y only (no foreign uploader, Book 03 / section 3.5 #6). Measured 10290 gzip at #338 (incl. 2-lens review fixes: writeValue trust-boundary validation + per-row focus management for WCAG 2.4.3 + success text status). #345 image thumbnails ([previewImages]: an object-URL-backed decorative minted lazily by an effect for non-invalid image/* files, revoked on remove/replace/destroy — no blob-URL leak) grew it to 12274 gzip; budget bumped 12288 to 15360 (~20% headroom). Barrel row stays 1280 (the +file-upload re-export shim fits its existing headroom: 1051/1280). The #276 bump caelum-carousel 11264->13312 covers two shipped follow-ups on carousel.ts, both shipping in the fesm: (1) responsive [responsiveOptions] (PR #433 — the CaeCarouselResponsiveOption interface + the matchMedia breakpoint effect + the activeOption/visibleCount/scrollCount rewire + JSDoc, which consumed the prior ~16% headroom without needing a bump), and (2) RTL awareness (PR #435 — the @angular/cdk/bidi Directionality inject + isRtl computed + the mirrored trackTransform + the indicator Left/Right arrow flip + the physical-border chevron with the .cae-carousel--rtl rotation-swap + JSDoc; @angular/cdk/bidi stays EXTERNAL, imported not inlined, as in cae-image-compare/cae-splitter) — measured ~11.2kb gzip, restoring ~16% headroom. The #287 bump caelum-galleria 9472->11520 covers content-agnostic projected item/thumbnail templates (#287): the CaeGalleriaItemDef + CaeGalleriaThumbnailDef directives + CaeGalleriaTemplateContext, NgTemplateOutlet pulled into both cae-galleria and its lightbox, and the itemTemplate threaded through the dialog data so fullscreen renders the same custom content — measured ~9.5kb gzip, ~15% headroom.
